children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to impair the enforcement of section 223 or 231 of title 47, chapter 71 (relating to obscenity) or 110 (relating to sexual exploitation of children) of title 18, or any other Federal criminal statute.

Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to affect in any way the Commission’s authority to bring enforcement actions under FTC Act for materially false or deceptive representations or unfair practices in commercial electronic mail messages.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This chapter supersedes any statute, regulation, or rule of a State or political subdivision of a State that expressly regulates the use of electronic mail to send commercial messages, except to the extent that any such statute, regulation, or rule prohibits falsity or deception in any portion of a commercial electronic mail message or information attached thereto.

children.heading: State law not specific to electronic mail
children.label: 2 State law not specific to electronic mail
A
children.text: State laws that are not specific to electronic mail, including State trespass, contract, or tort law; or

children.label: B
children.text: other State laws to the extent that those laws relate to acts of fraud or computer crime.

children.heading: No effect on policies of providers of Internet access service
children.label: c No effect on policies of providers of Internet access service
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to have any effect on the lawfulness or unlawfulness, under any other provision of law, of the adoption, implementation, or enforcement by a provider of Internet access service of a policy of declining to transmit, route, relay, handle, or store certain types of electronic mail messages.
